pattern: Turning per-item weights into a prefix-sum ladder lets a single uniform random draw plus a binary search pick indices proportionally to their weight in logarithmic time.
hints: Picking an index with probability proportional to its weight is like throwing a dart at a number line marked off in weighted segments.
Build a running total of the weights; the segment a random point in [1, total] lands in tells you the index.
Binary search the prefix-sum array for the first value >= your random draw — that index is your answer.

edge_inputs.description: single weight forces every pick to index 0
two heavily skewed weights (index 1 should dominate but index 0 must still be legal)
all equal weights across five buckets
maximum single weight at the constraint ceiling
a huge first weight next to a tiny last weight

stress.description: random weight vector with a burst of pickIndex calls
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 200)
    w = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['Solution']
    argv = [[w]]
    calls = max(1, min(size, 300))
    for _ in range(calls):
        ops.append('pickIndex')
        argv.append([])
    return [ops, argv]

complexity.time: O(log n) per pickIndex, O(n) to build
